team_composition.name: builder-frontend-core
team_composition.domain: infrastructure + shared screens (S-001, S-016, S-014, S-015)

team_composition.name: builder-frontend-admin
team_composition.domain: admin-panel interface (S-008, S-009, S-010, S-011, S-012, S-013, S-017)

team_composition.name: builder-backend-crud
team_composition.domain: CRUD domains: auth, user-management, menu, recipe, settings

team_composition.name: builder-backend-logic
team_composition.domain: complex domains: inventory, order-management, kitchen, payment, shift-management, reporting

team_composition.name: builder-sync
team_composition.domain: sync infrastructure: sync engine, conflict resolver, change tracker, PWA/service worker

tasks.subject: Implement frontend navigation, auth flow, and guard logic

description.goal: Implement sidebar/tab navigation contents, auth flow UI (login form, error handling, session check), guard logic (PinGateModal interaction, ShiftGuard redirect), and shared layout interiors for DashboardHubLayout, WaiterCashierLayout, KitchenLayout, AdminLayout.

infra: Navigation renders correctly for all roles (owner, waiter-cashier, chef)
Auth flow works: login form validates input, shows errors, redirects to dashboard on success
AuthGuard redirects unauthenticated users to login
RoleGuard blocks unauthorized role access
PermissionGuard checks user-specific permissions
PinGateModal shows numpad, verifies PIN via useAuthStore, blocks POS entry without PIN
ShiftGuard redirects to open shift page when no active shift for payment actions
All 4 layouts render correct navigation elements

tasks.active_form: implementing frontend navigation, auth flow, and guard logic

tasks.subject: Set up backend infrastructure with database schema and server

description.goal: Set up Express.js server with middleware stack, implement auth middleware (bcrypt-based local auth), configure Prisma client initialization, add base route structure, implement error handling middleware, and CORS configuration.

infra: Server starts on configured port
Prisma client connects to PostgreSQL
Auth middleware validates session tokens
Error handling returns structured JSON errors
CORS allows configured origins
Base route structure responds to health check

tasks.active_form: setting up backend infrastructure with database schema and server

tasks.subject: Implement sync engine with offline queue and conflict resolution

description.goal: Implement the sync queue for batched push/pull of IndexedDB changes to PostgreSQL, conflict resolution (last-write-wins for most entities, append-only for financial records), service worker for PWA offline support, and online status detection hook.

infra: Sync queue processes batched changes
Service worker registers and caches app shell
Online status hook detects connectivity changes
Conflict resolution applies last-write-wins correctly
Financial records (Transaction, Shift, VoidRecord) use append-only semantics
No data loss during offline-to-online transition

tasks.active_form: implementing sync engine with offline queue and conflict resolution

tasks.subject: Build auth and dashboard frontend (LoginPage, DashboardHubPage)

description.goal: Implement LoginPage with username/password form, error display, and post-login routing to DashboardHub. Implement DashboardHubPage with role-based menu grid, welcome header with user display name, shift status indicator, and quick info bar.

cross_domain_contracts.context: Cashier logs in (step 1), then verifies PIN for shift operations (step 3).
cross_domain_contracts.next_domain: shift-management opens shift after PIN verified

business_rules.rule: Each user must have a unique username
After login, ALL users are routed to the Main Dashboard Hub (S-016)
Dashboard Hub shows role-appropriate menu options
Failed login attempts are logged

F-009: Users can log in with username and password
All users are routed to Main Dashboard Hub after login
Dashboard Hub shows role-appropriate menu cards
Logged-in users name is displayed in the interface

tasks.active_form: building auth and dashboard frontend pages

tasks.subject: Build shift management frontend (OpenShiftPage, CloseShiftPage)

description.goal: Implement OpenShiftPage with PIN verification numpad, opening balance input, and shift confirmation. Implement CloseShiftPage with one-way closing lock banner, shift summary, line-by-line transaction report with print, cash reconciliation numpad, discrepancy indicator, mandatory notes for discrepancy, and close confirmation.

cross_domain_contracts.context: Cashier has logged in and verified PIN (auth steps 1, 3). Now opens shift with opening balance and later closes shift.

business_rules.rule: Cashier MUST verify PIN before opening a shift
Only one active shift per cashier at a time
Expected cash = opening_balance + cash_sales - cash_refunds_from_voids
Closing is a ONE-WAY session
If discrepancy exists, notes are MANDATORY
Closed shift data is immutable and logged for audit

F-015: Cashier sees Open Shift option on Main Dashboard Hub
Cashier must enter correct PIN before accessing shift opening
Cashier enters opening cash balance and confirms to start shift
During active shift, current shift info is visible in header
Cashier can initiate Close Shift -- this enters a one-way closing session
In closing session, cashier CANNOT navigate back to operations
Close Shift screen shows full shift summary and line-by-line transaction detail
Cashier can print the transaction report for physical tracing
System calculates and displays discrepancy with color indicator
If discrepancy exists, notes field is mandatory and close button is disabled until filled
If cash matches, closing completes directly
Owner can view shift history with all opening/closing data and discrepancies

tasks.active_form: building shift management frontend pages

tasks.subject: Build order list, create order, and table map pages

description.goal: Implement OrderListPage (S-002) with kitchen ready notification bar, active orders list with status badges and elapsed timers, cancel order confirmation modal. Implement CreateOrderPage (S-003) with table selector, promo banner, menu browser with category filter, order summary sidebar. Implement TableMapPage (S-005) with visual table grid and status indicators.

cross_domain_contracts.context: Waiter selects table, browses menu, creates order.
timing_notes: Stock deduction at order submission (D-001, D-030, D-064)
cross_domain_contracts.next_domain: kitchen receives order, inventory deducts stock

cross_domain_contracts.context: Kitchen marks order ready, waiter sees notification on S-002

business_rules.rule: Order must have at least one menu item
Each order is linked to a specific table
If a table already has an active (unpaid) order, cashier can ADD items to that existing order
Additional items are appended to the same order with Tambahan label
Order total recalculated automatically after adding items
Order can be cancelled before payment (status -> cancelled)
Discount applied before PPN: Grand Total = (Subtotal - Discount) * (1 + PPN rate)

F-001: Waiter can create a new order by selecting menu items and assigning to a table
If table already has active order, waiter sees option to Tambah Pesanan
Additional items are appended to existing order and sent to kitchen
Order total updates automatically after adding items
Order appears on Kitchen Display within 2 seconds of submission
Order total is auto-calculated based on menu item prices and quantities
Special notes are visible to the chef on Kitchen Display
F-010: Visual grid showing all tables with color-coded status
Waiter can tap a table to create or view its active order
Table status updates automatically when order is created/paid
Owner can configure total number of tables
F-017: Cashier can cancel an unpaid order from order list

tasks.active_form: building order list, create order, and table map pages

tasks.subject: Build payment and void transaction pages

description.goal: Implement PaymentPage (S-004) with order detail display, discount selector, payment method tabs (tunai/qris/e-wallet/transfer), numpad for cash, QR display for digital, success popup with print receipt, and void transaction modal with PIN gate and reason selection.

cross_domain_contracts.context: Order has been served, waiter initiates payment.
timing_notes: Stock deduction at order submission (D-001, D-030, D-064)
cross_domain_contracts.next_domain: order status updated to paid, table released, shift totals updated

cross_domain_contracts.context: Cashier finds paid order, verifies PIN, processes void.
cross_domain_contracts.next_domain: order voided, shift expected cash reduced, stock reversed

business_rules.rule: PPN calculation
Grand total calculation
Payment cannot be processed if amount paid is less than grand total
Transaction stores SPECIFIC payment method name, NOT generic Digital
Cashier MUST open a shift before processing any payment
PIN verification required before any void operation
Void reason is MANDATORY
Expected cash formula updated on void

F-003: Payment screen shows subtotal, PPN %, PPN amount, and grand total
Cashier can select payment method and input amount received
Change is calculated against grand total (inclusive PPN)
After payment: success popup with checkmark, summary, and Print Struk button
Cashier can print receipt from success popup
Cashier can tap Selesai to return to order list
Completed payment updates order status to paid
F-017: Cashier can void a paid transaction (requires PIN)
Voided amounts are reflected in shift expected cash calculation

tasks.active_form: building payment and void transaction pages

tasks.subject: Build kitchen display pages (KitchenQueuePage, RecipeViewerPage)

description.goal: Implement KitchenQueuePage (S-006) with kanban-style order queue (pending/cooking/ready columns), new order audio alert, elapsed timer per order, late order red highlight (>15 min), tambahan badge, single-tap status transitions, recipe quick access links. Implement RecipeViewerPage (S-007) with recipe header, ingredients list, cooking steps, and notes.

cross_domain_contracts.context: Order-management submits order (step 3), kitchen receives it in queue.
cross_domain_contracts.next_domain: Notification sent to waiter POS screen when ready

cross_domain_contracts.context: Chef marks order ready, waiter sees notification, waiter confirms served.
cross_domain_contracts.next_domain: order-management order status updated to served

business_rules.rule: New orders appear at the top of the queue with audio/visual alert
Chef taps to change status: pending -> cooking -> ready
When chef marks ready: notification sent to waiter/cashier POS screen immediately
Orders exceeding target time (15 min) are highlighted in red as late
Additional items (tambahan) from same table appear as new items in the SAME order card
Recipe is viewable from Kitchen Display per order item

F-002: Kitchen display shows all pending and cooking orders in queue format
Chef can update order status with a single tap
Recipe/cooking guide is accessible from each order item
When order is marked ready, waiter gets real-time notification on POS screen
Notification shows table number and items that are ready
Waiter can mark order as served from notification
Elapsed time counter visible on each order
Completed orders move to done section
F-006: Chef can open recipe guide from Kitchen Display with one tap on a menu item
Recipe shows ingredients, step-by-step instructions, and estimated cooking time

tasks.subject: Build admin dashboard, menu management, and recipe management pages

description.goal: Implement AdminDashboardPage (S-008) with KPI summary cards, revenue chart, peak hours heatmap, top selling items, payment method breakdown, shift performance table, and low stock alerts widget. Implement MenuManagementPage (S-009) with menu list table, category filter, add/edit form modal with image upload. Implement RecipeManagementPage (S-010) with recipe list, viewer panel, and editor form.

business_rules.rule: Report data updates in real-time as transactions are completed
Only completed (paid) orders are counted in revenue
Unavailable items are hidden from the order screen
Price changes take effect immediately for new orders
Recipe ingredients must reference inventory items for stock deduction

F-007: Owner can view todays sales summary on a dashboard
Dashboard shows total revenue, order count, and top-selling items
Owner can filter reports by date range
Data refreshes automatically without manual action
F-005: Owner can add, edit, and delete menu items
Menu items can be categorized and filtered
Availability toggle immediately affects order screen
Menu items can be linked to recipe guides and inventory ingredients
F-006: Owner can create and edit recipe guides for each menu item
Recipe shows ingredients, step-by-step instructions, and estimated cooking time

tasks.subject: Build inventory, transaction history, user management, and settings pages

description.goal: Implement InventoryManagementPage (S-011) with stock overview table, restock/adjust modals, movement history drawer. Implement TransactionHistoryPage (S-012) with advanced filter bar, summary stats, transaction detail modal with print, export to Excel. Implement UserManagementPage (S-013) with user list, create/edit form, permission checklist panel. Implement SystemSettingsPage (S-017) with PPN toggle/rate, payment method configs, discount management, restaurant info, receipt settings.

business_rules.rule: Stock deduction is triggered automatically when an order is confirmed
Only authorized users can manually adjust stock (restock or correction)
Only Owner can access the access control panel
Permission changes take effect immediately
Core permissions cannot be removed
PPN rate is configurable by owner in system settings
Changes to PPN rate apply to new transactions only, not retroactively
Discount applied BEFORE PPN calculation: Grand Total = (Subtotal - Discount) + PPN
Only one discount per order (prevent stacking)

F-004: Owner can view all inventory items with current stock levels
Stock auto-decreases when orders are placed based on recipe ingredients
System shows stock movement history (in/out log)
F-012: System shows a warning badge when any item is below threshold
Owner can set minimum threshold per inventory item
Low stock items are highlighted in the inventory view
Dashboard shows count of low-stock items
F-011: Transaction table shows: date/time, ID, table, Shift ID, PIC Kasir, subtotal, PPN, total, payment method
Transactions can be filtered by: date range, transaction ID, PIC Kasir, Shift ID, payment method, total range
All filters are combinable and have a reset button
Summary stats update with active filters
Each transaction shows full detail modal with PPN breakdown and Print Struk button
Filtered data can be exported to Excel (.xlsx)
Transaction data cannot be deleted by non-owner users
F-013: Export button on Transaction History generates Excel file
Loading indicator shown during export generation
F-008: Owner can view a list of all users with their current permissions
Owner can toggle each permission on/off via checkboxes
Changes apply immediately without requiring user to re-login
Default permissions are pre-set based on role but fully customizable
F-016: Owner can access POS Settings from admin panel or Dashboard Hub
Owner can set PPN rate percentage
F-018: Owner can create/edit/deactivate discount rules in settings
Active promos shown as banner cards on order creation screen
Discount picker shows all active discounts with calculated amount
Payment shows: Subtotal -> Diskon -> Subtotal setelah diskon -> PPN -> Grand Total

tasks.subject: Implement backend auth and user management APIs

description.goal: Implement auth endpoints (login, logout, verify-pin, session) and user management endpoints (CRUD users, get/update permissions). Local-first auth with bcrypt password/PIN hashing. Session token management via IndexedDB.

business_rules.rule: Each user must have a unique username
Failed login attempts are logged
Owner can set and reset PIN per user for shift operations
Only Owner can access the access control panel
Permission changes take effect immediately
Each role has default permissions that can be overridden per individual user
Core permissions cannot be removed

F-009: Users can log in with username and password (bcrypt comparison)
Session token returned on successful login
PIN verification works for shift/void/POS contexts
Owner can manage all user accounts (create, edit, deactivate, set PIN)
F-008: Owner can view a list of all users with their current permissions
Owner can toggle each permission on/off via checkboxes
Default permissions are pre-set based on role but fully customizable

tasks.active_form: implementing backend auth and user management APIs

tasks.subject: Implement backend menu, recipe, and settings APIs

description.goal: Implement menu CRUD endpoints (items + categories), recipe CRUD endpoints (with nested ingredients and steps), and settings endpoints (POS settings, e-wallet providers, discount management).

business_rules.rule: Unavailable items are hidden from the order screen
Price changes take effect immediately for new orders
Recipe ingredients must reference inventory items for stock deduction
Only Owner or authorized chef can edit recipes
PPN is calculated as: ppn_amount = subtotal * (ppn_rate / 100)
Changes to PPN rate apply to new transactions only, not retroactively
Discount applied BEFORE PPN calculation: Grand Total = (Subtotal - Discount) + PPN
Only one discount per order (prevent stacking)

F-005: Menu item CRUD operations work correctly
Availability toggle persists and takes effect
F-006: Recipe CRUD with nested ingredients and steps
F-014: Cannot delete category with assigned items
F-016: PPN toggle and rate configuration persists
Settings are readable by all authenticated users
F-018: Cannot delete discount applied to active orders

tasks.active_form: implementing backend menu, recipe, and settings APIs

tasks.subject: Implement backend inventory APIs with stock check and deduction

description.goal: Implement inventory CRUD endpoints, restock and manual adjustment endpoints with stock movement logging, stock movement history query, low stock alert query, and stock check endpoint for order submission validation. Implement stock status state machine (ok/low/critical based on quantity vs threshold).

cross_domain_contracts.context: Inventory is a side-effect target: order submission triggers stock deduction.
timing_notes: Stock deduction at order submission (D-001, D-030, D-064)

cross_domain_contracts.context: Order-management requests stock check before order submission.

business_rules.rule: Stock deduction is triggered automatically when an order is confirmed
steps: For each order item, look up recipe
For each recipe ingredient, deduct quantityRequired * orderItem.quantity from inventory
Update stockStatus based on new quantity vs minimumThreshold

business_rules.rule: Deduction amounts are based on recipe ingredient definitions
Stock cannot go below zero - system warns if insufficient
Only authorized users can manually adjust stock (restock or correction)

F-004: Stock auto-deduction on order works via recipe ingredients
Restock and manual adjustment create stock movement records
Stock status updates based on quantity vs threshold
F-012: Low stock endpoint returns items below threshold

tasks.active_form: implementing backend inventory APIs with stock check and deduction

description.goal: Implement order CRUD endpoints with state machine transitions, add-items endpoint for additional orders with Tambahan label, order status update with stock deduction side-effects, cancellation with stock reversal. cross_domain_contracts.context: Waiter selects table, browses menu, creates order.
timing_notes: Stock deduction at order submission (D-001, D-030, D-064)
cross_domain_contracts.next_domain: kitchen receives order, inventory deducts stock

business_rules.rule: Order must have at least one menu item
Each order is linked to a specific table
Stock deduction triggered on order submission
steps: Check recipe ingredients for each order item
Update stock status if below threshold

business_rules.rule: Order total recalculated automatically
When cancelling, reverse stock deductions via reversal StockMovement
steps: For each order item, look up recipe
Release table if no other active orders

business_rules.rule: Table status updates automatically when order is created/paid

F-001: Order creation with auto-subtotal calculation works
Additional items endpoint appends with Tambahan label
Order status transitions follow state machine
Stock warnings returned on order submission
F-010: Table status auto-updates on order lifecycle
F-017: Cancellation before payment restores table and stock

Kitchen display auto-removes served orders after confirmation

Only one active shift per cashier at a time
Expected cash = opening_balance + cash_sales - cash_refunds_from_voids
Closing is a ONE-WAY session
If discrepancy exists, notes are MANDATORY
Closed shift data is immutable and logged for audit
Only completed (paid) orders are counted in revenue

tasks.subject: Implement backend sync push/pull/status APIs

description.goal: Implement server-side sync endpoints: push (receive batched changes from client), pull (send changes since last sync timestamp), and status check. Integrate with Prisma for PostgreSQL persistence. Handle conflict detection and resolution (last-write-wins general, append-only for financial records).

business_rules.rule: Auto-sync to cloud/server when internet connection is available
No data loss during offline-to-online transition
Append-only semantics for financial records (Transaction, Shift, VoidRecord)

infra: Push endpoint receives and persists batched changes
Pull endpoint returns changes since last sync timestamp
Status endpoint returns sync state and pending counts
Conflict detection returns conflicted records
Financial records use append-only sync strategy

tasks.active_form: implementing backend sync push/pull/status APIs
